description: The International Phonetic Alphabet — one symbol per speech sound, each with a real pronunciation recording. Each item is tested both ways, tracked separately.
primer:
## How this set works
The **IPA** gives every distinct speech sound its own symbol, so any language can be written by sound. Each card has a real **🔊 recording** — tap it. A consonant is named by *voicing + place + manner* (e.g. [p] = voiceless bilabial plosive); a vowel by *height + backness + rounding* (e.g. [i] = close front unrounded). Each card is studied **both ways** and tracked separately — you only master it once you know it in both directions.

## Consonants (by manner)
- **Plosives:** p b · t d · k g · ʔ (glottal stop)
- **Nasals:** m · n · ŋ
- **Fricatives:** f v · θ ð · s z · ʃ ʒ · h
- **Affricates:** tʃ · dʒ
- **Approximants:** ɹ · j · w · l

## Vowels (front → back)
- **Close:** i (see) · y (Fr. 'u') · u (boot)
- **Near-close:** ɪ (sit) · ʊ (put)
- **Mid:** e · ə (schwa) · o
- **Open-mid:** ɛ (bed) · ʌ (cup) · ɔ (thought)
- **Open:** æ (cat) · a · ɑ (father)

## Why it's useful
Dictionaries, language courses and the alphabet decks all lean on IPA to pin down *exactly* how something sounds — independent of spelling. Every card's answer is named/charted here.
